newsSince: Material positive since June 9: on June 11, 2026 AXT subsidiary Beijing Tongmei signed a binding long-term InP wafer supply agreement with Nanjing Casela for a fixed quantity through 2027 totaling RMB173M (~$25.4M), with monthly deliveries — directly validating AI/CPO indium-phosphide substrate demand. Offsetting: revenue remains gated by Chinese export permits (a Q4-2025 permit drought erased a quarter), and the prior +377% YTD ran into CEO-distribution/InP-skepticism flush at the Needham conference.

sources.label: AXT subsidiary InP long-term supply deal (Jun 11)

verdicts.rankRationale: New June 11 binding InP supply deal (~$25.4M through 2027) is fresh validation of the CPO/optical-substrate bottleneck thesis, a genuine fundamental positive on this high-priority alpha screen. Tempered upgrade only: revenue is still gated by Chinese export permits and the name already ran +377% YTD.
verdicts.bull: Binding 2027 InP supply contract confirms AI-datacenter substrate demand; 6-inch InP capacity expanding.
verdicts.neutral: Demand strong but revenue hostage to China export-permit timing; valuation rich after huge run.
verdicts.bear: Export-permit drought can erase a quarter; +377% YTD, CEO distribution, single-bundle validation risk.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: InP substrate demand for AI optical/CPO links
